hint: This bustling metropolis is known for its neon lights, traditional temples, and being one of the largest metropolitan areas in the world.
The City of Light is famous for the Eiffel Tower, world-class museums, and being considered the romantic capital of Europe.
The city that never sleeps is home to the Statue of Liberty, Times Square, and is the financial heart of the United States.
This Australian city is famous for its Opera House, beautiful beaches, and iconic Sydney Harbour Bridge.
The Eternal City is home to the Colosseum, Vatican, and countless historical ruins from ancient times.
